rawText:
Thank you very much for having me, look forward to talking about the SpaceX Mars architecture.

And what I really want to try to achieve here is to make Mars seem possible, make it seem as though it's something that we can do in our lifetimes — and that you can go, and is there really a way that anyone can go if they wanted to? I think that's really the important thing.

So first of all, why go anywhere, right? I think there are really two fundamental paths. History is going to bifurcate along two directions: One path is we stay on Earth forever, and then there will be some eventual extinction event — I don't have an immediate doomsday prophecy — but eventually, history suggests that there will be some doomsday event.

The alternative is to become a space-faring civilization and a multi-planet species, which I hope you agree that is the right way to go.

So how do we figure out how to take you to Mars and create a self-sustaining city? A city that is not merely an outpost, but could become a planet in its own right, and thus we could become a truly multi-planet species.

If you look at the various planets in our solar system, there are really only two options that make sense. Venus is kind of a hot acid bath, so that's not really a good option. And then of the outer system planets, there are issues with distance and sunlight and the ability to get resources.

Now Mars, if we could warm it up, would have a very nice atmosphere. Mars is a fixer-upper of a planet. Mars is about half again as far from the sun as Earth, so it's still fairly close to the sun. It's a little cold, but we can warm it up. It has a very helpful atmosphere, which is primarily CO2 with some nitrogen and argon and a few other trace elements, which means that we can grow plants on Mars just by compressing the atmosphere.

The gravity on Mars is about 37% of that of Earth, so you'd be able to lift heavy things and bound around. The day is remarkably close to that of Earth — it's 24 hours and 37 minutes.

sentences.original: Thank you very much for having me, look forward to talking about the SpaceX Mars architecture.
sentences.translation: 초대해 주셔서 대단히 감사합니다. SpaceX 화성 아키텍처에 대해 이야기하게 되어 기대됩니다.

grammarElements.text: talking about the SpaceX Mars architecture
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (동명사구)

vocabulary.example: The software architecture is well-designed.
I look forward to meeting you.

expressions.usage: 강연이나 인터뷰 시작 시 인사로 사용하는 정형 표현
to 뒤에 동명사가 오는 것에 주의. 미래에 대한 기대를 표현

patterns.pattern: Thank you (very much) for [verb-ing] ..., look forward to [verb-ing] ...
patterns.explanation: 공식 발표나 강연의 전형적인 오프닝 패턴. 감사 + 기대의 조합.
examples: Thank you for inviting me, look forward to sharing our results.
Thank you for the opportunity, look forward to discussing this topic.

drillQuestions.question: Thank you very much for _____ me.
I look forward to _____ about the Mars architecture.

sentences.notes: 주어 "I"가 생략된 구어체 문장. "look forward to"에서 to는 전치사이므로 뒤에 동명사(talking)가 온다. to부정사와 혼동하기 쉬운 대표적인 표현.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 정형적 인사(formulaic opening) — 국제 학술대회 발표의 전형적인 공손한 시작

sentences.original: And what I really want to try to achieve here is to make Mars seem possible, make it seem as though it's something that we can do in our lifetimes — and that you can go, and is there really a way that anyone can go if they wanted to?
sentences.translation: 제가 여기서 정말로 달성하고자 하는 것은 화성을 가능하게 보이게 하는 것입니다. 우리 생애에 할 수 있는 일처럼 보이게 하는 것 — 그리고 여러분이 갈 수 있다는 것, 원한다면 누구나 갈 수 있는 방법이 정말 있느냐는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: what I really want to try to achieve here
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 관계대명사절 — 명사절)

grammarElements.text: make it seem as though it's something that we can do in our lifetimes
grammarElements.label: 보어 (make + 목적어 + 동사원형 — 사역)

grammarElements.text: is there really a way that anyone can go if they wanted to
grammarElements.label: 간접 의문문 (수사적)

vocabulary.example: She worked hard to achieve her goals.
This is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.
He acts as though he knows everything.

expressions.phrase: what I really want to achieve
expressions.usage: 발표 목표를 직접적으로 밝힐 때
시간적 범위를 현실적으로 한정하여 실현 가능성을 강조

patterns.pattern: What I really want to [verb] is to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 강조 구문(cleft sentence). 발표자의 핵심 의도를 명확히 전달하는 패턴.
examples: What I really want to show is that this technology works.
What I really want to explain is how we got here.

drillQuestions.question: _____ I really want to achieve here is to make Mars seem possible.
We can do this in our _____.

sentences.notes: 매우 긴 복합문. "want to try to achieve"에 to부정사가 3중으로 중첩되어 있다. "make Mars seem possible"은 사역동사 make + 목적어 + 동사원형 구조. 머스크 특유의 비공식적이고 즉흥적인 말투가 드러난다.

koreanAlignment.en: what I really want to try to achieve here
koreanAlignment.ko: 제가 여기서 정말로 달성하고자 하는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: is to make Mars seem possible
make it seem as though it's something
that we can do in our lifetimes
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 생애에 할 수 있는 일

koreanAlignment.en: that anyone can go if they wanted to

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 목표 선언(thesis statement) — 강연 전체의 핵심 목적을 청중에게 직접 선언

sentences.original: I think that's really the important thing.
sentences.translation: 저는 그것이 정말로 중요한 점이라고 생각합니다.

vocabulary.example: Education is important for everyone.

expressions.phrase: I think that's really the important thing
expressions.usage: 자신의 주장을 강조하며 마무리할 때

patterns.pattern: I think that's really the [adjective] thing.
patterns.explanation: 핵심 포인트를 강조하며 단락을 마무리하는 패턴. 구어적이고 자연스러운 표현.
examples: I think that's really the exciting part.
I think that's really the key issue.

drillQuestions.question: I think _____ really the important thing.

sentences.notes: 짧고 단순한 문장으로 앞의 긴 문장을 요약. 머스크가 자주 사용하는 구어적 강조 패턴으로, 핵심 메시지를 반복하여 청중의 기억에 남긴다.

sentences.original: So first of all, why go anywhere, right?
sentences.translation: 그래서 우선, 왜 어디든 가야 하는 걸까요, 그렇죠?

vocabulary.example: First of all, let me introduce myself.

expressions.usage: 청중의 잠재적 반론을 먼저 제기하여 논증을 시작하는 수사법

patterns.pattern: So first of all, [rhetorical question], right?
patterns.explanation: 수사적 질문으로 논의를 시작하는 패턴. "right?"를 덧붙여 청중과 대화하는 느낌을 줌.
examples: So first of all, why does this matter, right?
So first of all, what are we trying to solve, right?

drillQuestions.question: So first _____ all, why go anywhere?
Why go anywhere, _____?

sentences.notes: "why go anywhere"에서 주어(should we)가 생략된 비격식 수사적 질문. "right?"는 미국 영어에서 청중의 동의를 구하는 부가 의문의 역할. 머스크의 대화체 발표 스타일을 잘 보여준다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) — 청중의 잠재적 의문을 먼저 제기하여 논증의 출발점으로 삼음

sentences.original: I think there are really two fundamental paths.
sentences.translation: 저는 근본적으로 두 가지 길이 있다고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: there are really two fundamental paths
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어 — that 생략, 존재 구문)

vocabulary.example: This is a fundamental change in policy.
There are many paths to success.

expressions.usage: 자신의 분석을 겸손하게 제시할 때

patterns.pattern: I think there are [number] [adjective] [noun].
patterns.explanation: 핵심 논점의 수를 미리 제시하여 청중이 구조를 예상하게 하는 패턴.
examples: I think there are three key factors.
I think there are two possible solutions.

drillQuestions.question: I think there are two _____ paths.

sentences.notes: "two fundamental paths"는 이후 전개될 이분법적 논증 구조(지구에 머무르기 vs. 다행성 종이 되기)를 미리 예고하는 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: there are really two fundamental paths

sentences.original: History is going to bifurcate along two directions: One path is we stay on Earth forever, and then there will be some eventual extinction event — I don't have an immediate doomsday prophecy — but eventually, history suggests that there will be some doomsday event.
sentences.translation: 역사는 두 방향으로 갈라질 것입니다. 하나는 우리가 영원히 지구에 머무르는 것이고, 그러면 결국 멸종 사건이 일어날 것입니다 — 당장의 종말 예언이 있는 것은 아닙니다 — 하지만 결국, 역사는 어떤 종말적 사건이 있을 것임을 시사합니다.

grammarElements.text: One path is we stay on Earth forever
grammarElements.label: 주절 (첫 번째 경로)

grammarElements.text: there will be some eventual extinction event
grammarElements.label: 결과절 (미래)

grammarElements.text: I don't have an immediate doomsday prophecy
grammarElements.label: 삽입절 (대시로 구분 — 완화)

grammarElements.text: history suggests that there will be some doomsday event
grammarElements.label: 주절 (근거 제시)

vocabulary.example: The river bifurcates near the delta.
Many species face the risk of extinction.
Some people predicted a doomsday scenario.
The ancient prophecy came true.

expressions.usage: 미래의 선택지가 두 가지로 나뉠 때

expressions.phrase: I don't have an immediate doomsday prophecy
expressions.meaning: 당장의 종말 예언이 있는 것은 아닙니다
expressions.usage: 과장된 해석을 방지하기 위한 완화 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is going to [verb] along two directions: One ... the other ...
patterns.explanation: 이분법적 미래 시나리오를 제시하는 구조. 콜론(:)으로 설명을 도입.
examples: The market is going to split along two directions: growth or decline.
Technology is going to diverge along two paths: open or proprietary.

drillQuestions.question: History is going to _____ along two directions.
There will be some eventual _____ event.

sentences.notes: bifurcate는 라틴어 bi(둘) + furca(갈래)에서 유래한 고급 어휘. 대시(—)로 삽입된 "I don't have an immediate doomsday prophecy"는 자신의 주장이 공포 조장이 아님을 미리 방어하는 수사적 장치. 머스크의 논증 스타일이 잘 드러나는 핵심 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: History is going to bifurcate along two directions
One path is we stay on Earth forever
koreanAlignment.ko: 하나는 우리가 영원히 지구에 머무르는 것이고

koreanAlignment.en: there will be some eventual extinction event
I don't have an immediate doomsday prophecy
koreanAlignment.ko: 당장의 종말 예언이 있는 것은 아닙니다

koreanAlignment.en: history suggests that there will be some doomsday event
koreanAlignment.ko: 역사는 어떤 종말적 사건이 있을 것임을 시사합니다

pronunciationNotes: prophecy: /ˈprɑːfəsi/ — "프라퍼시", prophecy(명사)와 prophesy(동사)의 발음 차이 주의
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 이분법(dichotomy) — 인류의 미래를 두 가지 선택지로 단순화하여 논증의 긴급성을 부여

sentences.original: The alternative is to become a space-faring civilization and a multi-planet species, which I hope you agree that is the right way to go.
sentences.translation: 대안은 우주를 항해하는 문명과 다행성 종이 되는 것이며, 여러분도 이것이 올바른 방향이라는 데 동의하시길 바랍니다.

grammarElements.text: to become a space-faring civilization and a multi-planet species
grammarElements.label: 보어 (to부정사)

grammarElements.text: which I hope you agree that is the right way to go
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (which — 앞 내용 전체를 수식)

vocabulary.example: We need to consider the alternatives.
Becoming a space-faring civilization is a grand goal.
Humans are a remarkably adaptable species.
Ancient civilizations left remarkable architecture.

expressions.usage: 선택지 중 정답을 제안할 때
청중의 동의를 유도하는 완곡한 설득 표현

patterns.pattern: The alternative is to [verb] ..., which I hope you agree is the right way to go.
patterns.explanation: 두 번째 선택지를 제시하면서 청중의 동의를 유도하는 패턴.
examples: The alternative is to invest in renewable energy, which I hope you agree is the right way to go.
The alternative is to rethink our approach, which I believe is the better path.

drillQuestions.question: The _____ is to become a space-faring civilization.
I hope you agree that is the right _____ to go.

sentences.notes: space-faring은 seafaring(항해하는)에서 파생된 합성어로, 우주 항해를 의미. multi-planet은 하이픈으로 연결된 합성 형용사. "which I hope you agree"는 청중을 자신의 편으로 끌어들이는 수사적 전략.

koreanAlignment.en: that is the right way to go

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 청중 참여 유도(audience engagement) — "I hope you agree"로 청중을 논증의 참여자로 끌어들임

sentences.original: So how do we figure out how to take you to Mars and create a self-sustaining city?
sentences.translation: 그러면 어떻게 여러분을 화성에 데려가고 자급자족 도시를 만들 방법을 알아낼까요?

grammarElements.text: how to take you to Mars
grammarElements.label: 의문사 + to부정사 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: We need to figure out a solution.
The colony needs to be self-sustaining.

expressions.usage: 청중과 함께 문제 해결 과정을 시작할 때
"you"를 직접 사용하여 청중을 참여시키는 표현

patterns.pattern: How do we figure out how to [verb] ...?
patterns.explanation: 중첩된 how 의문문. 방법을 알아내는 방법을 묻는 메타적 질문 패턴.
examples: How do we figure out how to reduce costs?
How do we figure out how to scale this technology?

drillQuestions.question: How do we figure _____ how to take you to Mars?
We need to create a _____-sustaining city.

sentences.notes: "figure out how to"에서 how가 두 번 나타나는 중첩 구조. "you"는 청중 전체를 지칭하여 화성 여행이 청중 자신의 일처럼 느끼게 하는 효과. self-sustaining은 self(스스로) + sustaining(유지하는)의 합성어.

koreanAlignment.en: So how do we figure out
how to take you to Mars

pronunciationNotes: figure: /ˈfɪɡjər/ — "피겨", g 다음의 y 소리에 주의

sentences.original: A city that is not merely an outpost, but could become a planet in its own right, and thus we could become a truly multi-planet species.
sentences.translation: 단순한 전초기지가 아니라 그 자체로 하나의 행성이 될 수 있는 도시, 그래서 우리가 진정으로 다행성 종이 될 수 있는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: that is not merely an outpost
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (부정)

grammarElements.text: but could become a planet in its own right
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (대조 — but)

grammarElements.text: and thus we could become a truly multi-planet species
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (결과 — thus)

vocabulary.example: This is not merely a theory.
The Antarctic research station is a remote outpost.
She is a talented artist in her own right.
collocations: a force in its own right

vocabulary.example: The project was delayed, and thus the budget increased.

expressions.usage: 기대 이상의 목표를 제시할 때
독립적인 가치나 지위를 가진다고 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: [Noun] that is not merely [A], but could become [B]
patterns.explanation: 목표의 규모를 확대하는 패턴. 단순한 것을 넘어 더 큰 비전을 제시.
examples: A project that is not merely research, but could become a global initiative.
A school that is not merely a building, but could become a community hub.

drillQuestions.question: A city that is not _____ an outpost.
It could become a planet in its own _____.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 앞 문장의 "self-sustaining city"를 부연 설명하는 동격 구조. "not merely... but"은 "not only... but also"와 유사하나 merely가 더 격식적. "in its own right"는 독립적 가치를 강조하는 관용 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: A city that is not merely an outpost
but could become a planet in its own right
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 자체로 하나의 행성이 될 수 있는

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층법(climax) — "전초기지 → 행성 → 다행성 종"으로 비전의 규모를 점진적으로 확대

sentences.original: If you look at the various planets in our solar system, there are really only two options that make sense.
sentences.translation: 우리 태양계의 다양한 행성들을 살펴보면, 실제로 말이 되는 선택지는 두 가지뿐입니다.

grammarElements.text: If you look at the various planets in our solar system
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if)

vocabulary.example: There are various ways to solve this problem.
Mars is the fourth planet in our solar system.
Does this plan make sense to you?

expressions.usage: 청중에게 함께 분석하자고 제안할 때
논리적으로 말이 되는 선택지를 한정할 때

patterns.pattern: If you look at [X], there are really only [number] options that make sense.
patterns.explanation: 분석을 통해 선택지를 좁히는 패턴. 논증에서 결론을 향해 범위를 줄여나가는 기법.
examples: If you look at the data, there are really only three explanations that make sense.
If you look at the market, there is really only one strategy that makes sense.

drillQuestions.question: If you _____ at the various planets, there are only two options.
There are only two options that _____ sense.

sentences.notes: "make sense"는 "타당하다, 이치에 맞다"라는 의미의 매우 빈번한 관용 표현. options가 복수이므로 관계대명사절의 동사도 make(복수형).

koreanAlignment.en: If you look at the various planets
there are really only two options

sentences.original: Venus is kind of a hot acid bath, so that's not really a good option.
sentences.translation: 금성은 말하자면 뜨거운 산성 목욕탕 같은 곳이라, 그다지 좋은 선택지가 아닙니다.

grammarElements.text: kind of a hot acid bath
grammarElements.label: 보어 (비유적)

grammarElements.text: so that's not really a good option
grammarElements.label: 결과절 (so)

vocabulary.example: Venus is the second planet from the sun.
Acid rain damages buildings and vegetation.
It's kind of difficult to explain.

expressions.phrase: kind of a hot acid bath
expressions.usage: 복잡한 과학적 사실을 유머러스한 비유로 설명할 때

expressions.phrase: that's not really a good option
expressions.usage: 부드러운 어조로 부정할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is kind of [vivid metaphor], so [negative conclusion]
patterns.explanation: 유머러스한 비유로 복잡한 사실을 설명하고, 간결한 결론으로 이어지는 패턴.
examples: The old system is kind of a dinosaur, so it needs to be replaced.
That approach is kind of a dead end, so we need a new strategy.

drillQuestions.question: Venus is _____ of a hot acid bath.
That's not _____ a good option.

sentences.notes: 머스크 특유의 유머 감각이 드러나는 문장. 금성의 표면 온도가 약 462°C이고 황산 구름으로 뒤덮여 있다는 과학적 사실을 "hot acid bath"라는 생생한 비유로 단순화. "kind of"는 구어체에서 매우 빈번하게 쓰이는 완화 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: Venus is kind of a hot acid bath
koreanAlignment.ko: 금성은 뜨거운 산성 목욕탕 같은 곳입니다

koreanAlignment.en: so that's not really a good option

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 유머(humor) — 과학적 사실을 일상적 비유로 풀어 청중의 웃음을 유도

sentences.original: And then of the outer system planets, there are issues with distance and sunlight and the ability to get resources.
sentences.translation: 그리고 외부 태양계 행성들의 경우, 거리, 햇빛, 자원 확보 능력에 문제가 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: with distance and sunlight and the ability to get resources
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (with — 문제의 내용)

vocabulary.example: The outer planets are much farther from the sun.
The country is rich in natural resources.
There are several issues to address.

expressions.usage: 문제점을 나열할 때
능력이나 가능성의 제한을 언급할 때

patterns.pattern: Of the [category], there are issues with [A] and [B] and [C]
patterns.explanation: 특정 범주에 속하는 항목들의 문제점을 나열하는 패턴.
examples: Of the remaining candidates, there are issues with experience and availability.
Of the alternative fuels, there are issues with cost and efficiency.

drillQuestions.question: _____ the outer system planets, there are issues.
There are issues _____ distance and sunlight.

sentences.notes: 3개의 명사(distance, sunlight, the ability to get resources)가 and로 병렬 연결. 마지막 항목만 명사구(the ability to get resources)로 더 복잡한 구조를 가지고 있어 끝이 무겁게 처리됨(end-weight principle).

koreanAlignment.en: and the ability to get resources

sentences.original: Now Mars, if we could warm it up, would have a very nice atmosphere.
sentences.translation: 화성은, 따뜻하게 할 수 있다면, 매우 좋은 대기를 가지게 될 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: if we could warm it up
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (가정법 과거 — if + could)

vocabulary.example: We need to warm up the engine before driving.
The Earth has a nitrogen-oxygen atmosphere.

expressions.phrase: if we could warm it up
expressions.usage: 가정법으로 미래 가능성을 제시할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject], if we could [verb], would [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 가정법 과거(2nd conditional). 삽입된 조건절이 주절 사이에 놓여 강조 효과를 줌.
examples: Earth, if we could reverse climate change, would be much healthier.
The project, if we could secure funding, would succeed.

drillQuestions.question: Mars, if we _____ warm it up, would have a nice atmosphere.
Mars _____ have a very nice atmosphere.

sentences.notes: 가정법 과거(second conditional) 구조. 조건절(if we could warm it up)이 주어와 동사 사이에 삽입되어 괄호처럼 기능. "Now"는 시간이 아닌 담화 전환의 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: if we could warm it up
would have a very nice atmosphere
koreanAlignment.ko: 매우 좋은 대기를 가지게 될 것입니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(transition) — "Now"로 금성/외행성에서 화성으로 청중의 관심을 전환

sentences.original: Mars is a fixer-upper of a planet.
sentences.translation: 화성은 손볼 곳이 있는 행성입니다.

vocabulary.meaning: 손볼 곳이 있는 집(비유: 개선이 필요한 것)
vocabulary.example: They bought a fixer-upper and renovated it themselves.

expressions.usage: 원래 부동산 용어를 행성에 적용한 유머. 화성이 완벽하지는 않지만 개선 가능하다는 의미

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is a [metaphorical noun] of a [noun].
patterns.explanation: "X of a Y" 구조로 비유를 만드는 패턴. 일상 용어를 전혀 다른 맥락에 적용.
examples: This project is a marathon of a task.
That exam was a monster of a test.

drillQuestions.question: Mars is a _____-upper of a planet.

sentences.notes: fixer-upper는 원래 "수리가 필요한 집"을 뜻하는 미국 부동산 속어. 이것을 행성에 적용한 것이 머스크의 유머 포인트. "a X of a Y" 구조(예: a hell of a day)는 영어 고유의 비유적 패턴.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 비유(metaphor) — 부동산 용어를 행성에 적용하여 화성을 친근하고 접근 가능한 대상으로 제시

sentences.original: Mars is about half again as far from the sun as Earth, so it's still fairly close to the sun.
sentences.translation: 화성은 지구보다 태양에서 약 1.5배 더 멀지만, 여전히 태양에 꽤 가깝습니다.

grammarElements.text: about half again as far from the sun as Earth
grammarElements.label: 보어 (배수 비교 — as...as)

grammarElements.text: so it's still fairly close to the sun
grammarElements.label: 결과절 (so)

vocabulary.example: The new model costs half again as much as the old one.
It's fairly easy to learn.

expressions.usage: 배수 비교의 독특한 표현. half again = 1 + 0.5 = 1.5배
예상보다 나쁘지 않다는 긍정적 뉘앙스를 전달

patterns.pattern: [A] is [multiplier] as [adjective] as [B], so ...
patterns.explanation: 배수 비교 패턴. as...as 구문 앞에 배수를 넣어 비율을 표현.
examples: The new building is twice as tall as the old one.
This route is three times as long as the shortcut.

drillQuestions.question: Mars is about half again as _____ from the sun as Earth.
It's still _____ close to the sun.

sentences.notes: "half again as far as"는 영어 특유의 배수 비교 표현으로, "1.5배 더 먼"이라는 뜻. 한국어로 직역하기 어렵다. 화성과 태양 사이의 평균 거리는 약 2.28억 km로, 지구(약 1.50억 km)의 약 1.52배.

koreanAlignment.en: Mars is about half again as far from the sun as Earth
koreanAlignment.ko: 화성은 지구보다 태양에서 약 1.5배 더 멉니다

koreanAlignment.en: so it's still fairly close to the sun

sentences.original: It's a little cold, but we can warm it up.
sentences.translation: 좀 춥기는 하지만, 따뜻하게 만들 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: but we can warm it up
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (대조 — but)

vocabulary.example: I'm a little tired today.

expressions.phrase: it's a little cold, but we can warm it up
expressions.meaning: 좀 춥지만 따뜻하게 만들 수 있다
expressions.usage: 문제를 축소하고 해결 가능성을 강조하는 낙관적 표현

patterns.pattern: It's a little [problem], but we can [solution].
patterns.explanation: 문제를 축소(understatement)하고 즉시 해결책을 제시하는 패턴. 낙관적 톤을 유지.
examples: It's a little expensive, but we can find the funding.
It's a little complex, but we can simplify it.

drillQuestions.question: It's a _____ cold, but we can warm it up.

sentences.notes: 화성의 평균 온도는 약 -60°C로, "a little cold"는 극단적인 절제법(understatement). 이러한 유머가 청중의 웃음을 유도하면서도 "해결 가능한 문제"라는 메시지를 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: but we can warm it up

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 절제법(understatement/litotes) — 화성의 극한 추위(-60°C)를 "a little cold"로 축소하여 유머와 낙관을 동시에 전달

sentences.original: It has a very helpful atmosphere, which is primarily CO2 with some nitrogen and argon and a few other trace elements, which means that we can grow plants on Mars just by compressing the atmosphere.
sentences.translation: 매우 유용한 대기를 가지고 있는데, 주로 CO2와 약간의 질소, 아르곤, 그리고 몇 가지 미량 원소로 구성되어 있어서, 대기를 압축하는 것만으로 화성에서 식물을 기를 수 있다는 뜻입니다.

grammarElements.text: which is primarily CO2 with some nitrogen and argon and a few other trace elements
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (which — atmosphere를 수식)

grammarElements.text: which means that we can grow plants on Mars just by compressing the atmosphere
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (which — 앞 내용 전체를 수식)

vocabulary.example: The diet consists primarily of vegetables.
Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere.
Trace elements are essential for plant growth.
Compressing the gas increases its pressure.

expressions.usage: 앞선 사실로부터 결론이나 함의를 도출할 때
"단지 ~하는 것만으로"라는 의미로 간편함을 강조

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has [feature], which is [composition], which means that [implication].
patterns.explanation: 특성 설명 → 구성 → 함의로 이어지는 3단계 논증 패턴. which가 연쇄적으로 사용.
examples: The soil has a unique composition, which is rich in minerals, which means that crops can grow easily.

drillQuestions.question: The atmosphere is _____ CO2.
We can grow plants just _____ compressing the atmosphere.

sentences.notes: which가 두 번 연속 사용된 복잡한 문장. 첫 번째 which는 atmosphere를, 두 번째 which는 앞 절 전체를 선행사로 취함. "just by -ing"에서 just는 "단지"의 의미로 과정의 단순함을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: It has a very helpful atmosphere
and a few other trace elements
which means that we can grow plants on Mars
koreanAlignment.ko: 화성에서 식물을 기를 수 있다는 뜻입니다

sentences.original: The gravity on Mars is about 37% of that of Earth, so you'd be able to lift heavy things and bound around.
sentences.translation: 화성의 중력은 지구의 약 37%이므로, 무거운 것을 들어 올리고 이리저리 뛰어다닐 수 있을 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: lift heavy things and bound around
grammarElements.label: 보어 (두 동사의 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The gravity on the Moon is about one-sixth of Earth's.
The children were bounding around the playground.
Can you help me lift this box?

expressions.usage: that은 앞에 나온 명사(gravity)의 반복을 피하기 위한 대명사
would + be able to로 가정적 미래의 능력을 표현

patterns.pattern: [A] is about [percentage] of that of [B], so you'd be able to ...
patterns.explanation: 비교 수치를 제시하고, 그 결과로 가능해지는 일을 설명하는 패턴.
examples: The cost is about 50% of that of the original, so you'd be able to afford it.
The weight is about 30% of that of steel, so you'd be able to carry it easily.

drillQuestions.question: The gravity on Mars is about 37% of _____ of Earth.
You'd be able to lift heavy things and _____ around.

sentences.notes: "that of Earth"에서 that은 gravity를 대신하는 지시대명사. 이 용법은 한국어에 없어 자주 혼동되는 구조. bound는 "경계"가 아닌 "뛰다"의 의미로 사용. 화성에서의 생활을 재미있게 묘사하여 청중의 흥미를 유발.

koreanAlignment.en: is about 37% of that of Earth

sentences.original: The day is remarkably close to that of Earth — it's 24 hours and 37 minutes.
sentences.translation: 하루의 길이는 놀랍도록 지구와 비슷합니다 — 24시간 37분입니다.

grammarElements.text: remarkably close to that of Earth
grammarElements.label: 보어 (that = the day)

vocabulary.example: The two languages are remarkably similar.
The temperature is close to freezing.

expressions.usage: 두 대상의 유사성이 예상 밖으로 높을 때

patterns.pattern: [A] is remarkably close to that of [B] — [specific value].
patterns.explanation: 유사성을 강조하고, 대시 뒤에 구체적 수치로 뒷받침하는 패턴.
examples: The price is remarkably close to that of last year — just $5 more.
The performance is remarkably close to that of the original — 98% accuracy.

drillQuestions.question: The day is _____ close to that of Earth.
The Martian day is 24 hours and 37 _____.

sentences.notes: "that of Earth"에서 that은 앞의 "The day"를 대신하는 지시대명사. 대시(—) 뒤에 구체적 수치를 제시하여 "remarkably close"를 증명. 화성의 하루(sol)가 지구와 거의 같다는 사실은 화성 정착의 실용적 이점.

sentences.original: The issue that we have today is that if you look at a Venn diagram, there's no intersection of sets of people who want to go and can afford to go.
sentences.translation: 오늘날 우리가 가진 문제는, 벤 다이어그램을 보면, 가고 싶은 사람들과 갈 여유가 되는 사람들의 교집합이 없다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: The issue that we have today
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사절 포함)

grammarElements.text: if you look at a Venn diagram
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if)

grammarElements.text: there's no intersection of sets of people
grammarElements.label: 주절 (존재 구문 부정)

grammarElements.text: who want to go and can afford to go
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who — people을 수식)

vocabulary.example: A Venn diagram shows the overlap between two groups.
The intersection of the two sets is empty.
Not everyone can afford to buy a house.

expressions.usage: 수학적 용어를 빌려 두 그룹의 겹침이 없음을 표현
경제적 능력이 있음을 표현

patterns.pattern: The issue is that there's no [noun] of [group A] who [want] and [can] ...
patterns.explanation: 문제를 "두 조건의 교집합 부재"로 정의하는 분석적 패턴.
examples: The issue is that there's no overlap of students who are interested and who have the qualifications.
The problem is that there's no intersection of companies that are willing and that have the capacity.

drillQuestions.question: There's no _____ of sets of people who want to go.
Not everyone can _____ to go to Mars.

sentences.notes: 수학적 개념(벤 다이어그램, 교집합)을 비즈니스 논증에 활용하는 머스크 특유의 분석적 사고 방식. that이 두 번 사용되어 있는데, 첫 번째 that은 issue를 수식하는 관계대명사, 두 번째 that은 보어절을 이끄는 접속사.

koreanAlignment.en: The issue that we have today is
that if you look at a Venn diagram
there's no intersection of sets of people

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 분석적 비유(analytical metaphor) — 수학적 개념(벤 다이어그램)을 도입하여 문제를 시각적·논리적으로 명확하게 제시
